title: Purchase Modal with Dynamic WordPress Content
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want upgrade prompts to display marketing-controlled content, so that the messaging is always current and compelling without requiring code changes.
description:
Convert the purchase/upgrade modal from static in-app content to dynamic content served from DataToolsPro.com via WordPress REST API.

A new WordPress custom post type 'Upgrade Windows' holds the HTML content for each upgrade prompt. Each product feature that triggers an upgrade has an associated slug. The modal loads content dynamically from the WordPress REST API using the slug, displays the title and body content, and renders a button (with label from metadata) linking to Stripe checkout.

This allows marketing to experiment with messaging, images, and CTAs without developer involvement. Four upgrade buttons are configured per the Product Upgrade Paths document.
acceptance_criteria: Upgrade modal content loads dynamically from WordPress REST API
Each upgrade prompt uses a feature-specific slug to fetch content
WordPress custom post type 'Upgrade Windows' is created and functional
Marketing can edit modal content without code changes
Modal displays title, HTML body, and a button linking to Stripe checkout
All four upgrade paths from the product document are implemented
ux_changes: Upgrade modals display dynamic content from WordPress CMS instead of hardcoded text. Title and button labels are customizable.
middleware_changes: Service to fetch upgrade content from WordPress REST API by slug and render in the modal.
api_changes: Inbound: WordPress REST API for custom post type 'Upgrade Windows' with slug-based lookup.
implementation_notes: WordPress meta field for button text proved difficult to expose via REST API. Developer created posts with titles used as modal headers. Slug-based lookup enables different content per feature upgrade path.
comment_summary: Developer created the custom post type and dynamic content loading. Had difficulty exposing meta fields via REST API. Implemented the four upgrade buttons per the product upgrade paths document.
